reviewsSummary.note: Re-run review: only fix-color-contrast reviewed (fixing prior add-color-accents failures). Prior passing reviews (fix-dropdown-sizing) unchanged.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated color-system.md to replace old failing hex values (#FF3300, #00AA00) with corrected canonical values (#CC2900, #007700). Removed 'Known WCAG AA Failures' section (issues resolved). Added note about the old values being corrected in commit 4bbb562.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the Brutalist design rules color palette entry from 'black (#000) / white (#fff) + #0055FF accent' to list all four accent colors now in use: #0055FF (blue/primary), #CC2900 (orange/alternative), #007700 (green/success), #CC0000 (red/error). This prevents workers from treating #0055FF as the only valid accent and from introducing ad hoc colors or reverting to the old failing values.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The palette was expanded to four colors in add-color-accents (commit 0fea9f1) and corrected in fix-color-contrast (commit 4bbb562). Without documentation update, future workers may add more colors or use the old uncorrected hex values. Flagged by both the add-color-accents and fix-color-contrast reviewers.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Make 'npm run lint' the first mandatory verification step, before typecheck and tests. Two workers (add-color-accents and fix-color-contrast) skipped lint during verification. The skill already requires it, but workers are consistently skipping it. Moving it to first position makes the omission more visible.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both add-color-accents handoff and fix-color-contrast handoff list commandsRun as: typecheck, test, curl health. Both omit lint. The fullstack-worker SKILL.md requires lint. This is a recurring deviation across two workers on the same milestone.

rejectedObservations.observation: CSS text-truncation pattern is repeated in multiple places and could be documented as a utility class convention.
rejectedObservations.reason: Carried over from round 1 rejection — still not actionable without broader refactoring of the inline CSS architecture.
